title: Count Objects with One-to-One Correspondence
description: Determine the total number of objects in a group by counting each object once.
condition: Given a visible set of objects arranged in any configuration.
response: Count each object once and state the total number.
label: Production / Skill
example: Given 7 dots arranged in a circle, count: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 → total = 7.

title: Identify the Larger of Two Numerals
description: Determine which of two written numerals represents the greater quantity.
condition: Given two written numerals and asked which number is more.
response: Select the numeral with the larger value.
label: Principle / Model
example: Given 4 and 6, since $6 > 4$, select 6 as the number that is more.

title: Identify the Set with More Objects
description: Compare two groups of objects and determine which group has more items.
condition: Given two sets of objects and asked which has more.
response: Select the set with the greater number of objects.
label: Principle / Model
example: If one group shows 6 objects and another shows 8 objects, since $8 > 6$, select the group with 8 objects.

title: Match Quantity to Written Numeral
description: Associate a counted set of objects with its corresponding written numeral.
condition: Given a set of objects and multiple written numerals.
response: Select or connect the numeral that represents the counted quantity.
label: Rule / Plan
example: If a set contains 8 objects, match it to the numeral 8.

title: Recognize Written Numerals (0–10)
description: Identify a written numeral symbol and associate it with its spoken number name.
response: State or internally recognize the corresponding number name and value.
label: Concept
example: When shown the symbol 7, recognize it as "seven."

title: Write Numeral to Represent Quantity
description: Produce a written numeral that represents a counted quantity.
condition: Given a counted total of objects and asked to write the number.
label: Production / Skill
example: After counting 7 dots, write the numeral 7.
